title: Laurent Mekies: Relishing Red Bull Challenge | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
Red Bull Racing were at a crossroads when Laurent Mekies took over as Team Principal halfway through 2025. After a difficult first half of the season, should they continue working on their 2025 car or write that year off and prioritise the new regulations for 2026?

title: How Jacques Villeneuve Shook Up F1 | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The son of a motorsport legend, a racer since childhood, Formula 1 was Jacques Villeneuve's destiny. In 1996, the young Canadian arrived at his first Grand Prix and immediately proved he belonged there by taking pole position.

In his rookie season Villeneuve won races, went wheel-to-wheel with Michael Schumacher and took Williams teammate Damon Hill to a final race title-decider. 30 years on, he sits down with Tom Clarkson to remember how he shook up the sport.

title: 2026 F1 Chinese GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: New regs or not - McLaren-Mercedes in the race (or not): nothing could detract from the enormity of this day in Shanghai - from the first GP win for Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli. From the pole, he seized the race lead from Ferrari's Sir Lewis Hamilton on lap two - and was thereafter never headed.  Lewis re-applied pressure after the pit stops; and team-mate, George Russell, was up their in P2, pushing hard, as the race unfolded.  Kimi, though, was unperturbed. title: Dan Towriss: The ‘Passion & Drive’ Behind Cadillac F1 Team | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The newest team in Formula 1 are here to compete. Cadillac have earned the respect of their rivals. Now they’re pushing to get faster.

CEO Dan Towriss takes Tom Clarkson back to the start of the story - how he and his TWG Motorsports company first became involved in discussions about F1, conversations with General Motors and Cadillac, the work to build a team, choosing their race drivers and making their F1 debut in Australia.

Sergio Perez and Valtteri Bottas are the first Cadillac F1 drivers.

title: Juan Pablo Montoya's record-breaking lap of Monza | 2004 Italian GP
description:
Colombia's Juan Pablo Montoya averaged an eye-watering 259.83 km/h when setting his pole position lap around Monza back in 2004.

This stood as a record until 2018, when it was narrowly eclipsed by Kimi Raikkonen with an average speed of 263.587km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q5_fo16E4LU

Lewis Hamilton then lowered the record further in 2020, with a lap that averaged 264.363 km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f1PtJV0vIs

Five years later, Max Verstappen went even faster, with an avera
